page: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 128 vCPUs mapped to 64 physical cores with multithreading enabled, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system is configured with 128 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier performance in multi-core processing, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 172,613.68 and a PassMark CPU score of 89,602.52. It demonstrates strong capabilities in memory bandwidth, reaching 8,132.17 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and excels in cryptographic operations and ray tracing. However, single-core performance and text processing benchmarks fall into the average tier.

The primary architectural tradeoff of the n2d-highcpu-128 is its low memory-to-CPU ratio, which limits its suitability for memory-intensive databases or large-scale in-memory processing. This is reflected in its weak performance during large-scale LLM text generation, where it achieves only 2.67 tokens/sec on a 70B model. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance maximizes compute density per resource for highly parallelizable, CPU-bound tasks that do not require massive memory footprints or local NVMe storage.

This server type is highly suited for compute-heavy workloads such as software compilation, ray tracing, static web serving, and high-throughput Redis operations. It is also effective for prompt processing in small to medium language models, though it is not recommended for memory-heavy databases or large-model text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 128 vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it offers a lean 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU. Benchmarks show top-tier multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and strong cryptographic execution, though single-core metrics and large-scale LLM text generation remain average to weak. This resource profile makes the instance highly cost-efficient for parallel, CPU-bound workloads like software compilation, ray tracing, and high-throughput Redis deployments, while presenting a clear tradeoff for memory-intensive applications.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 is a compute-optimized server with 128 vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2d-highcpu-128 features 128 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, optimized for high-compute parallel workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute instance featuring 128 dedicated vCPUs, optimized for parallel processing, software compilation, and high-throughput workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 128-vCPU AMD EPYC 7B13 processor
Configured with 128 GB RAM, providing 1.0 GB of memory per vCPU
Top-tier multi-core CPU and cryptographic benchmark performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis operations
Underperforms on memory-intensive tasks and large LLM text generation
